column3: const repository = await this._gitService.openRepository(repositoryUri);","I'll review this change with a focus on correctness, type safety, and downstream impact.

column1: The change itself is simple, but it introduces a contract mismatch that needs to be addressed before merging.

The resolver’s `resolve` method previously returned `undefined` when no repository URI was available, signaling that changeset resolution was not possible. Now it returns an empty array, which semantically means “no changes detected.” This changes the meaning of the return value and may affect callers.

The method signature is `Promise<IChatSessionFileChange2[] | undefined>`. Returning `[]` violates the union type because `undefined` can no longer occur. This is misleading and may cause TypeScript errors for callers that properly check for `undefined`. The signature should be updated to `Promise<IChatSessionFileChange2[]>` (remove `| undefined`).
Without this change, developers will assume the function can still return `undefined` and may write unnecessary guards or incorrect logic.
Any caller that relies on `undefined` to distinguish between “no repository” and “repository exists but no changes” may break. For example:
if (changes) { // previously false for undefined, now true for []

Now `changes` will always be truthy (an array), so the `if` block will always execute. If the caller proceeds to iterate, an empty array is harmless, but if it performs other logic based on the truthiness, it could lead to unexpected behavior.
All callers of `resolve` must be audited to ensure they handle the new contract correctly.

The commit message or PR description is absent. Without that, it’s unclear why this change was made. If the goal is to fix a crash caused by `undefined` propagation, the better fix might be to update callers to handle `undefined` gracefully, not to change the contract silently. If the goal is to simplify the interface, then the type should be updated accordingly.
- Confirm that all callers of `resolve` are compatible with always receiving an array (e.g., they use `changes.length` or `for...of` without an `undefined` check).
- If any caller relied on `undefined` to signal an error or skip processing, adjust that logic accordingly.
- Provide a clear commit message explaining the motivation and verifying caller safety.
Once these are addressed, the change is safe to merge.",0.0,20.82765221595764,2681,2026-07-14T08:43:01.134503+00:00

The version change is a patch bump, so no breaking API changes are expected. However, please:
- Quickly check the [`tmp` release notes](https://github.com/raszi/node-tmp/releases/tag/v0.2.7) to confirm there are no unexpected changes in behavior.
- Run the `test/integration/browser` tests locally to ensure the updated `tmp` version doesn’t affect the temporary file/directory handling in the browser tests.

The `package-lock.json` changes look correct (new version, resolved URL, and integrity hash). Ensure this was generated by running `npm install` in the `test/integration/browser` directory (not manually edited). If any other dependencies were unintentionally changed, they would have appeared in the diff.

The `@types/tmp` dependency is pinned at `0.1.0`. While unlikely to be an issue for a patch release, it’s worth verifying that the types still match the runtime API. -# Visual Studio Code - Open Source (""Code - OSS"")

-This repository (""`Code - OSS`"") is where we (Microsoft) develop the [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com) product together with the community. Not only do we work on code and issues here, but we also publish our [roadmap](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Roadmap), [monthly iteration plans](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Iteration-Plans), and our [endgame plans](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Running-the-Endgame). This source code is available to everyone under the standard [MIT license](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/LICENSE.txt).

-[Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com) is a distribution of the `Code - OSS` repository with Microsoft-specific customizations released under a traditional [Microsoft product license](https://code.visualstudio.com/License/).

-[Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com) combines the simplicity of a code editor with what developers need for their core edit-build-debug cycle. It provides comprehensive code editing, navigation, and understanding support along with lightweight debugging, a rich extensibility model, and lightweight integration with existing tools.

-Visual Studio Code is updated monthly with new features and bug fixes. You can download it for Windows, macOS, and Linux on [Visual Studio Code's website](https://code.visualstudio.com/Download). To get the latest releases every day, install the [Insiders build](https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders).
-There are many ways in which you can participate in this project, for example:
-* [Submit bugs and feature requests](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues), and help us verify as they are checked in
-* Review the [documentation](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-docs) and make pull requests for anything from typos to new content.
-If you are interested in fixing issues and contributing directly to the code base,
-please see the document [How to Contribute](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/How-to-Contribute), which covers the following:
-* [The development workflow, including debugging and running tests](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/How-to-Contribute#debugging)
-* Connect with the extension author community on [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-discussions/discussions) or [Slack](https://aka.ms/vscode-dev-community)
-* Follow [@code](https://x.com/code) and let us know what you think!
-See our [wiki](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Feedback-Channels) for a description of each of these channels and information on some other available community-driven channels.

-Many of the core components and extensions to VS Code live in their own repositories on GitHub. For example, the [node debug adapter](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-node-debug) and the [mono debug adapter](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-mono-debug) repositories are separate from each other. For a complete list, please visit the [Related Projects](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Related-Projects) page on our [wiki](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki).

-VS Code includes a set of built-in extensions located in the [extensions](extensions) folder, including grammars and snippets for many languages. Extensions that provide rich language support (inline suggestions, Go to Definition) for a language have the suffix `language-features`.
